frameworks.name: cortical state modulation
frameworks.one_line: Transient changes in cortical power spectrum during cognitive acts

frameworks.name: beta-band oscillation
frameworks.one_line: 12-30 Hz modulation indexes perceptual decision processing in visual cortex

frameworks.name: perceptual decision-making
frameworks.one_line: Converting graded perceptual signals into categorical format for behavior

core_question: Does the beta-band modulation in visual cortex during an elementary perceptual decision reflect the decision itself rather than motor, arousal, or secondary processes?
key_assumptions: Beta-band power modulation indexes a top-down cortical signal
Target appearance/disappearance constitutes an elementary visual decision
MEG can dissociate motor, arousal, and decision components
manipulations: Overt button-press report vs silent counting of perceptual events
Compared beta-band against pupil arousal, fixational eye movements, gamma-band
key_findings: Target disappearance produced beta-band suppression; reappearance produced beta-band enhancement
Beta-band modulation occurred for both button-press and silent counting reports
Pupil-linked arousal, fixational eye movements, and gamma-band responses were not necessary for the beta-band modulation
interpretations: Beta-band modulation is a top-down signal tied to perceptual decision processing, not motor execution
It reflects converting graded perceptual signals into a categorical format underlying flexible behavior
